flashcards.definition: Core finance principle: money available now is worth more than the same amount in the future due to its earning potential.
The current worth of a future sum of money discounted at a given rate; answers 'What is $X in N years worth today?'
The value of a current amount after earning interest for N periods; FV = PV × (1+r)^n.
The interest rate used to determine present value; reflects opportunity cost and risk.
A series of equal payments made at regular intervals (mortgage payments, retirement withdrawals, insurance premiums).
